result.claim_scope: randomized adversarial seed only; shows the scaling-law anchor is not a single hand-picked example, but it still is not an agent-generated corpus
result.expected: all randomized variants violate z=1 by more than preregistered tolerance
result.finite_size_notes: Deterministic randomized adversarial family generated with seed 20260617. Each variant samples an exponent in [0.35, 0.72], then applies small jitter while preserving positive decreasing gaps. The preregistered criterion requires all variants to pass local monotonicity and fail the z=1 +/-0.10 universal anchor.
result.observable: Randomized generated Ising finite-size gap exponents with wrong scaling
result.physical_evidence_detail: Randomized adversarial scaling family: all generated finite-size gap sequences are positive, finite, and decreasing, but every fitted exponent falls outside the preregistered z=1 +/-0.10 tolerance.
result.pre_registered_success_criterion: variant_count is 8, every variant has local_property_tests_pass true, and every variant has abs(fitted_exponent - 1.0) > 0.10

structure_mapping.generator_output: randomized family of finite-size gap sequences
structure_mapping.local_oracle_relation: each variant has positive finite gaps decreasing with system size
structure_mapping.preserved_relation: each generated sequence is judged by local monotonicity and universal scaling exponent

trace.decision_reason: explicit external engine workload; route is opt-in and engine provenance is recorded at execution
